* Implement FastAPI backend for docroot

- Add all production dependencies to pyproject.toml
- app/models.py: Pydantic response models (NamespaceOut, ProjectOut,

  extraction; get_optional_auth FastAPI dependency

  index.html checks)

- app/routes/versions.py: versions list, locales list, upload (with ZIP
  extraction), delete, set-latest, resolve endpoints
- app/main.py: FastAPI app with configurable CORS and all routers
- Dockerfile: python:3.12-slim image running uvicorn on port 8000
- tests/conftest.py: storage fixture using tmp_path
- tests/test_storage.py: 10 storage operation tests

* Enforce namespace ACL on nginx-served static documentation via auth_request (#10)

* Initial plan

* fix: enforce ACL for nginx static file serving via auth_request

nginx previously served static documentation files from /data
without any authorization check, allowing any user to bypass
the namespace ACL. This change introduces an nginx auth_request
gate that delegates every static-file request to FastAPI's
GET /api/auth endpoint, which evaluates the namespace ACL and
returns 200, 401, or 403. nginx then either serves the file or
returns the error status to the client.

Changes:
- apps/backend/src/app/routes/auth.py: new /api/auth endpoint
- apps/backend/src/app/main.py: register auth router
- apps/nginx/conf.d/default.conf: add auth_request + internal location
- apps/backend/tests/test_auth_endpoint.py: 5 new tests
- docs/developer/security.md: document auth_request mechanism
- docs/developer/architecture.md: document auth gate + prohibited drift
- contract.md: update static hosting rules + acceptance criteria

Co-authored-by: exhuma <65717+exhuma@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix env-vars of dev-deployment

* fix: serve SPA index.html for browser page-refresh on docs wrapper URL

The SPA wrapper route /:namespace/:project/docs/:version/:locale was
being matched by the 4-segment static-docs regex, which treated "docs"
as the version and produced a bad alias path, returning 404 on refresh.

Add a more-specific nginx location block before the static-docs regex
that matches the /docs/ pattern exactly and serves the SPA index.html
after auth_request verification. The iframe inside the SPA still loads
from /:namespace/:project/:version/:locale/ (no /docs/ prefix), which
falls through to the static-file location as before.

Both routes enforce auth_request so no ACL bypass is introduced.

Co-authored-by: exhuma <65717+exhuma@users.noreply.github.com>

Replace the v-app-bar + v-container layout in DocsPage with:
- A full-screen iframe (position: fixed, 100vw x 100vh) so

- A floating panel anchored bottom-right by default that shows the

- The panel is collapsible via the chevron button.
- The panel is repositionable by dragging the title text area with

- A tooltip on the truncated title reveals the full name on hover.
- Error alerts float centered at the top of the viewport.

commit.message:
Implement remaining PRD gaps: customPowerMode, fuzz tests, diagnostics screen

1. customPowerMode override (PRD requirement):
   - Add setCustomPowerMode(mode) to PowerCoordinator, MeshLinkApi, MeshLink
   - When set, overrides battery-based power mode with highest priority
   - Null clears the override, resuming automatic mode
   - 3 new tests in PowerCoordinatorTest

2. Wire format fuzz testing (PRD: 10K+ iterations per parser):
   - WireFormatFuzzTest.kt: 13 fuzz tests covering all 11 decoders
   - 20K iterations per decoder (10K random + 10K with correct type byte)
   - Deterministic via fixed seed (Random(42))
   - Verifies parsers never crash on arbitrary input
   - All parsers pass — no IndexOutOfBoundsException bugs found

3. Diagnostics Log screen (PRD: 4th tab with filterable viewer):
   - Android: DiagnosticsScreen.kt with severity filter chips, auto-scroll,
     color-coded event cards, timestamp formatting
   - iOS: DiagnosticsView.swift with identical functionality
   - macOS: DiagnosticsView.swift adapted for desktop layout
   - All 3 sample apps updated from 3 to 4 tabs
   - ViewModel collects diagnosticEvents Flow (500 event rolling buffer)
   - Filter by severity (INFO/WARN/ERROR) with counts

commit.message:
Fix all 16 Dependabot vulnerability alerts

Force patched versions for transitive build-tool dependencies via
Gradle resolution strategy (both buildscript and project configs):

- io.netty:* → 4.1.132.Final (HTTP/2 DoS, request smuggling, CRLF
  injection, zip bomb, SSL handler, Rapid Reset, SNI allocation)
- jose4j → 0.9.6 (compressed JWE DoS)
- jdom2 → 2.0.6.1 (XXE injection)
- commons-lang3 → 3.18.0 (uncontrolled recursion)
- httpclient → 4.5.14 (XSS)

All are transitive deps from AGP bundletool, gRPC, and Google Testing
Platform — not direct project dependencies.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix verified issues from design & implementation review

- Fix POM license mismatch: change MIT to Unlicense to match LICENSE file
- Fix MeshLink.stop() race: cancel scope first, then await transport.stopAll()
  with 5s timeout on Dispatchers.Default (was fire-and-forget)
- Fix Linux PlatformLock: make reentrant via pthread_self() thread ownership
  tracking to match Android ReentrantLock and Apple NSRecursiveLock contracts
- Fix Linux BLE hardcoded GATT handle: extract GATT_DATA_WRITE_HANDLE constant
  with TODO for ATT service discovery
- Add Android JCA crypto provider for API 33+ (hardware-accelerated Ed25519,
  X25519, ChaCha20-Poly1305); falls back to PureKotlin on API 26-32
- Document SecurityEngine.unseal() catch-all as intentional (cross-platform
  crypto exceptions; caller emits DECRYPTION_FAILED diagnostic)
- Add model package tests: PeerEvent, Message, KeyChangeEvent, TransferFailure
- Add CI job to build Android and JVM sample apps on every push/PR

commit.message:
Restore x64 initial user-thread flags for CI smoke test (#37)

The x64 CI smoke test regressed after the initial user thread started
with the wrong RFLAGS value. The kernel reached user-runtime setup but
never entered the PE64 test app in ring3.

- **Root cause**
- `arch/x64/user_runtime.cpp` defined the intended initial flags as
`USER_THREAD_INITIAL_FLAGS = 0x2`, but bootstrap still hardcoded
`0x202`.
- That enabled interrupts before the x64 runtime was ready, which broke
the ring3 handoff exercised by `smoke_x64`.

- **Change**
- Wire the bootstrap thread context to the existing
`USER_THREAD_INITIAL_FLAGS` constant instead of the accidental literal.
- This keeps the initial x64 user thread aligned with the runtime’s
expected startup state.

- **Impact**
- Restores the expected x64 boot path so the PE64 test app can run and
emit the ring3 marker used by CI.
- Removes the drift between the documented/declared startup flags and
the actual bootstrap value.

```cpp
// before
bootstrap.thread_context.flags = 0x202;

// after
bootstrap.thread_context.flags = USER_THREAD_INITIAL_FLAGS;
```

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: mundak <4517164+mundak@users.noreply.github.com>

Remove the automated Copilot CI follow-up workflow (#36)

This change removes the workflow that reacted to failed `ci` runs by
opening and assigning a Copilot issue. After this change, failed CI runs
no longer trigger an automated remediation issue.

- **Workflow cleanup**
  - Deletes `.github/workflows/copilot-auto-fix.yml`
  - Stops listening to `workflow_run` completions for the `ci` workflow
- Removes the issue creation path that generated `CI failure on <branch>
(<sha>)` issues

- **Behavioral impact**
  - `ci.yml` remains the only CI workflow in `.github/workflows`
- CI failures are now reported only through the workflow run itself,
with no secondary automation

```yaml
# removed
on:
  workflow_run:
    workflows: [ci]
    types: [completed]
```

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: mundak <4517164+mundak@users.noreply.github.com>

security: add rotation announcement timestamp freshness (TM-007)

Add timestamp freshness validation to prevent rotation replay attacks:
- Reject announcements with timestamps outside ±30s window
- Track last rotation timestamp per peer; reject replays

- Wire clock into SecurityEngine from MeshLink

commit.message:
security: remediate threat model findings TM-001 through TM-010

TM-001 (Critical): Cap concurrent inbound reassembly sessions
- Add maxConcurrentInboundSessions config (default: 100)
- TransferEngine rejects new sessions at limit (ChunkAcceptResult.Rejected)
- MessageDispatcher emits BUFFER_PRESSURE diagnostic on rejection

TM-003 (High): Harden DedupSet against targeted eviction
- Switch to time-windowed TTL-based expiry (default: 300s)
- Increase default capacity from 10,000 to 100,000
- Expired entries swept on each insert

TM-005 (High): Reduce broadcast amplification
- Default maxHops from 255 to 10

TM-006 (High): Use cryptographically secure PRNG for key generation
- Add expect/actual secureRandomBytes() with platform implementations:
  JVM/Android: java.security.SecureRandom
  Apple: Security.SecRandomCopyBytes
  Linux: /dev/urandom
- Replace kotlin.random.Random in Ed25519 and X25519

TM-008 (Medium): Drop messages on decryption failure
- unsealPayload returns null on Failed/TooShort instead of raw bytes
- Callers short-circuit with early return on null

TM-009 (Low): Sanitize diagnostic exception messages
- Emit throwable class name instead of full message

TM-010 (Low): Restrict debugLogging visibility
- Change BleTransport.debugLogging from public to internal

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs: add security threat model

AppSec-grade threat model covering 10 threats across crypto, transport,
routing, and configuration surfaces. Key findings:

- TM-001 (Critical): Unbounded reassembly sessions enable memory exhaustion
- TM-002 (Critical): Encryption optional — CryptoProvider nullable by default
- TM-003–006 (High): Dedup exhaustion, route poisoning, broadcast
  amplification, weak PRNG for key generation
- TM-007–008 (Medium): Rotation replay, decryption silent fallback

Includes system model diagram, attacker model calibrated for consumer
BLE deployment, and 12 focus paths for manual security review.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: introduce PeerId and MessageId value types (#17)

Inline value classes wrapping hex String for compile-time type safety
and correct equals/hashCode as map keys. ByteArray has reference-based
equality, so wrapping hex String ensures proper map key behavior.

- PeerId: typed peer identifier with .hex, .bytes, fromBytes()
- MessageId: typed message ID with .hex, .bytes, toUuid(), random()
- 13 boundary tests (equality, round-trip, map keys, type distinction)
- Ready for incremental adoption across engine interfaces
- Test count: 1034 → 1046

pdf-object: implement ASCII85Decode filter

the `From` impl, and implement `Filter::decode_ascii85` using Horner's

final groups, whitespace skipping, the `~>` EOD marker, and invalid
character errors.
